title: Living in Boulder vlog 9 -  7 -  2017: Lactantius, started Mountain Screen Captures

enrichment.summary: Kyle recaps his September 7th day in Boulder: getting his car repaired at a dealership, troubleshooting his Garmin watt meter, completing screen captures of mountains accessible from Boulder for a cycling project, and reading Lactantius's Divine Institutions. He also discusses his plan to calibrate his watt meter the next day and track his cycling power output.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: One of the most fascinating books you could read — Lactantius argues the pagan gods were just historical people, not real gods
Excited about it as an easy starting point for bike riding; sees it as a long-term project worth doing
Frustrated but determined to resolve it before fully committing to training

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday Kyle got up at 6 AM because he had to do chores and returned home at around 4:30 PM.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle called Garmin to get his watt meter fixed so he could start riding his bike.

claims.subject: Garmin
claims.claim: Garmin helped Kyle fix his watt meter by guiding him through a setup process and clicking some settings.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle picked up his car with only 15 minutes left before the place closed.

claims.subject: Kyle's car
claims.claim: Kyle's car had a problem where it was buzzing loudly due to a low-wheel pressure warning light, a short-circuited wire, and an oil light.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle was relieved that the dealership figured out the car issue for $100-$185.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle finished Google screen captures and is now ready to piece together the statements.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle created 400 mountains that can be climbed from Boulder, inspired by the idea 'all roads lead to Rome.'

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle mentioned that climbing these mountains will take a long time because they involve 200-mile trips.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is glad he has something easy to start working on his bike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle was listening to YouTube videos about a room and forgot to bring his MP3 player.

claims.subject: Tantius
claims.claim: Kyle finds Tantius's book 'Divine Institutions' fascinating, describing it as a critique of gods and a historical chronology.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle wants to memorize Tantius's work to incorporate it into his timeline.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to call Garmin tomorrow to fix his bike's lot display issue.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle will spend a day riding his bike to record data for his chart.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle has a crystal-level chart page with all the lots needed for different cases.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is losing weight quickly and is now 105 pounds lighter than his previous weight.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to sleep after being awake for almost 24 hours.

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder, V-Log. I guess for the day of September 7th, because it's 525 a.m.
Let's see, so what did I do? Yesterday I got up at 6, because I had to do chores, and I got back home at like 4.30,
And I remember, you know, got in the shower and came out and immediately called Garmin, because I wanted to see if I could get my wattmeter fixed so I could start riding my bike.
So, yeah, they helped me walk through the thing, and there was this, like, the setup thing, and then there was this window he'd never seen before,
And then we clicked some stuff, and then he's like, okay, so you're good to go now, it's all fixed, so I was like, yay.
And then I went over to pick up my car, and I only had, you know, like 15 minutes left to do that, or 45 minutes to get there, because they closed at 6.
I managed to call Garmin right before they closed at 5, and I was on there for like 15, 20 minutes.
So, yeah, I went and picked up my car, and my car had this problem where it was buzzing all the time, like really loud.
So, the low oil pressure, like warning light, the wire was short-circuited, and the oil light and the buzzer was on all the time, really annoying.
But luckily for me, he figured it out.
The guy used to work at the dealership, so I was glad he figured it out, and it was only $185.
So, then I got my car, came back, and yeah, so today I finished Google doing the screen captures of Strava, all the segments.
So, now the other, the next step would be to piece, piece it together.
But before that, I wanted to do screen captures of all the mountains.
I've, I created 400 mountains that you can climb from Boulder.
It's kind of like that idea, all roads lead to Rome.
Well, if you look at all the mountains coming up, roads coming up from Boulder to the mountains, it's kind of like that.
So, and then I started, yeah, so I was doing those mountains, and that's going to take a really long time, though,
Because they're really long trips, you know, like 200-mile trips.
If you can't do it with a road bike, you know, people can try to do it with electric bicycles.
But, yeah, that's going to be a while.
That'll be, whoo, I don't even want to think about how long that's going to take,
But that's something I can easily do on my bicycle.
So, I'm glad that I have something super easy to get started working on my bike.
And then, you know, once I get a little bit into it, I'll be able to do anything on my bike, whatever, on my website.
Just like I could have all along, but the problem is you sleep more, so you can't get quite as much done.
But, yeah, so I got started on this, figuring out exactly, I had to finish the stuff that I already prepared to, you know, I wasn't quite done.
And so, what was I listening to?
I was, I was listening, during the day I was just listening to YouTube stuff about Rome.
But, because I forgot to bring my MP3 player.
But, at home I was listening to Lactantius, Divine Institutions, off my website.
And that's one of the most fascinating books that you could possibly read.
It's only, like, 115 pages, but he basically, he's basically, like, the guy in, like, the 300s.
He was like, there are no gods.
The gods are just people.
Come on, you guys.
God is God.
So, yeah, he was talking about history and chronology of history, which is, I can use, I want to try to memorize, you know,
So I can plug into my website, I mean, my timeline, whenever I get around to it.
But, yeah, check that guy out, Lactantius.
And, yeah, so, yeah, I'm passing out.
I was working until I cracked, and besides, it's like 5.30, so it'll get light soon, so I definitely should get to sleep.
Yeah, and then, when I get up, I'm going to call Carmen.
Oh, yeah, so I forgot to finish the story.
So, as I was riding my bike to pick up my car, there were no watts.
So, I actually broke it.
I made it from having only 60% of what the real watts are to not showing any watts at all.
So, I'm going to call Carmen tomorrow and see if we can figure this out and get my watt thing fixed.
And once I do that, I'm going to abandon what I'm working on now, and I'm going to spend, like, an entire day not riding the bike just there.
Well, actually, I will be riding the bike because I have to do the recordings.
I'm going to ride the bike for, like, a minute at a certain cadence on a certain gear, and I'm going to record what the watt says and plug that in to my chart that I actually already have.
It's on my bike racer level chart page that has all the watts you need to do for the different cadences and the different speed for all the gears that I have, which is the 3450 by 1128.

I guess it's the standard compact gear, and, yeah, that'll be awesome to know because then I can maybe even print it up and put it on the front there and know exactly how strong I am at any given moment while riding on the rollers.
Um, and or how good of a biker I am because I weight, I'm weighing myself.
I was only, like, 185 last time I weighed myself, so I'm losing weight pretty quickly.
Um, so, anyways, I think that's it.
If I'm talking, I'm getting up almost 24 hours, I better get to sleep.
Peace out.
